name: 11407-mcp-integer-coercion
_purpose: Visual evidence for warpdotdev/warp#11407. The fix lives in `coerce_integer_args` (a private helper that runs before MCP tool calls hit the wire). Visual evidence is code-level: drive a terminal in warp-oss to `cat` the PANW test schema (proves the test exercises the exact shape from the issue body) and then run the unit test with `--nocapture` so the recording shows the assertions pass — i.e. the walker successfully coerced `0.0 / 100.0 / 1730419200000.0` to `0 / 100 / 1730419200000` at all three nested positions (object.object.int, object.object.array.oneOf.int).
_safety: Read-only commands: `cat` and `cargo test`. No agent credits consumed. No state mutation outside cargo's target dir.
_phases: A: cat the PANW test schema so the recording shows the JSON Schema shape
B: run cargo test --nocapture against the panw_audit_management_repro test
C: result frame stays on screen while last caption renders

steps.text: cd /tmp/warp-10596 && git branch --show-current && git rev-parse --short HEAD && echo && echo '=== PANW test schema (object.object.array.oneOf.integer) ===' && sed -n '/fn panw_audit_management_repro_coerces_all_integers/,/^}/p' app/src/ai/blocklist/action_model/execute/call_mcp_tool_tests.rs | sed -n '1,40p'
